classification: network security / packet capture and analysis tool (CLI)
short_description: A command-line packet capture and analyzer that uses libpcap to sniff, filter, display, and save network traffic in pcap/pcapng formats for security investigation and troubleshooting.
overview_notes: tcpdump is a long-standing, cross-platform packet analyzer primarily for Unix-like systems. It captures packets via the libpcap API, applies Berkeley Packet Filter (BPF) expressions in kernel or user space, decodes many protocols, and outputs human-readable summaries or writes capture files. It is widely used in network security workflows for incident response, intrusion analysis, policy validation, and performance diagnostics. It is free software under a BSD-style license and is commonly bundled in operating systems and security distributions. ([github.com](https://github.com/the-tcpdump-group/libpcap?utm_source=openai))
what_it_is_notes:
- A CLI packet capture tool that reads from network interfaces or existing capture files and can write packet data to disk. ([man7.org](https://man7.org/linux/man-pages/man8/tcpdump.8.html?utm_source=openai))
- Built atop libpcap (or Npcap on Windows), which provides a portable capture API and BPF-based filtering. ([github.com](https://github.com/the-tcpdump-group/libpcap?utm_source=openai))
- Uses the pcap (and, via modern libpcap, pcapng) capture file formats, interoperable with Wireshark and other tools. ([man7.org](https://man7.org/linux/man-pages/man5/pcap-savefile.5.html?utm_source=openai))
how_it_works_notes:
- Packet capture: tcpdump opens a network interface via libpcap, optionally enabling promiscuous mode, and receives frames from the OS capture mechanism (e.g., PF_PACKET on Linux). ([github.com](https://github.com/the-tcpdump-group/libpcap?utm_source=openai))
- Filtering: User-provided expressions are compiled to BPF bytecode and executed (typically in kernel space) to limit traffic before user-space processing. ([man7.org](https://man7.org/linux/man-pages/man7/pcap-filter.7.html?utm_source=openai))
- Parsing/decoding: tcpdump dissects many link-layer and upper-layer protocols and prints concise summaries; it can also save raw packets to files without decoding. ([man7.org](https://man7.org/linux/man-pages/man8/tcpdump.8.html?utm_source=openai))
- File formats: Reads/writes pcap and, when supported by linked libpcap, pcapng; these formats are widely supported across analyzers. ([man7.org](https://man7.org/linux/man-pages/man5/pcap-savefile.5.html?utm_source=openai))
- Privilege handling: On many OSes, capture requires elevated privileges; tcpdump can drop privileges after initializing capture (e.g., -Z). ([man7.org](https://man7.org/linux/man-pages/man8/tcpdump.8.html?utm_source=openai))
inputs: Live network interfaces (e.g., Ethernet, loopback, wireless) accessible via libpcap/Npcap.
Capture filters expressed in BPF syntax (e.g., host, net, port, protocol).
Configuration parameters such as snapshot length, buffer size, link-layer type selection, timestamp precision, and privilege drop user.
Existing capture files in pcap/pcapng formats to read and analyze.
outputs: Human-readable packet summaries on standard output for quick inspection.
Capture files in pcap or pcapng for archival and offline analysis.
Optional post-rotation actions and multi-file rolling capture sets for long-running jobs.
core_concepts: libpcap/Npcap as the capture API and portability layer. ([github.com](https://github.com/the-tcpdump-group/libpcap?utm_source=openai))
Berkeley Packet Filter (BPF) expression syntax and kernel/user-space execution. ([man7.org](https://man7.org/linux/man-pages/man7/pcap-filter.7.html?utm_source=openai))
pcap and pcapng capture file formats and interoperability with Wireshark. ([wireshark.org](https://www.wireshark.org/docs/wsdg_html_chunked/ChWorksCaptureFiles.html?utm_source=openai))
Promiscuous mode and its effect on visibility on switched networks.
Snapshot length (snaplen) and buffering impacting loss and performance. ([stackoverflow.com](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/39866757/how-to-resolve-tcpdump-dropped-packets?utm_source=openai))
Privilege management and post-initialization privilege drop. ([manpages.debian.org](https://manpages.debian.org/unstable/tcpdump/tcpdump.8.en.html?utm_source=openai))
typical_workflow: Define a focused BPF capture filter to constrain traffic volume at the source (e.g., hosts, ports, protocols). ([man7.org](https://man7.org/linux/man-pages/man7/pcap-filter.7.html?utm_source=openai))

Start a live capture on the relevant interface(s), choosing appropriate snapshot length and buffers to balance fidelity and performance. ([stackoverflow.com](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/39866757/how-to-resolve-tcpdump-dropped-packets?utm_source=openai))
Optionally enable rolling capture files/rotation for long-running monitoring and to bound disk usage. ([man7.org](https://man7.org/linux/man-pages/man8/tcpdump.8.html?utm_source=openai))
Write packets to pcap/pcapng for later deep analysis in Wireshark, Zeek, or other tools. ([wireshark.org](https://www.wireshark.org/docs/wsdg_html_chunked/ChWorksCaptureFiles.html?utm_source=openai))

Review capture statistics (received/filtered/dropped) to assess capture quality and adjust filters/buffers. ([stackoverflow.com](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/39866757/how-to-resolve-tcpdump-dropped-packets?utm_source=openai))

Drop privileges after device open for safer long-running operation, if supported in the environment. ([manpages.debian.org](https://manpages.debian.org/unstable/tcpdump/tcpdump.8.en.html?utm_source=openai))
use_cases: Incident response and threat hunting by capturing indicators of compromise or command-and-control traffic at chokepoints.
Validation of firewall/IDS/IPS policies by confirming permitted/blocked traffic on specific interfaces or spans.
Malware/sandbox analysis to observe network behaviors and protocol usage.
Network troubleshooting for latency, retransmissions, DNS issues, and MTU/fragmentation anomalies.
Baseline creation and anomaly detection support by exporting curated captures to SIEM/NDR pipelines.
limitations: Encrypted traffic (TLS, SSH, IPsec, DoH/DoT) limits payload visibility; metadata may still be useful.

High-throughput links can incur packet loss without careful filtering, buffering, and storage planning. ([stackoverflow.com](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/39866757/how-to-resolve-tcpdump-dropped-packets?utm_source=openai))

NIC/OS offloading features (GRO/LRO/TSO) can alter capture semantics, showing coalesced segments or checksum anomalies; disabling offloads may be required for wire-accurate analysis. ([meet-unix.org](https://meet-unix.org/2017-02-19-tcp-lso.html?utm_source=openai))

Captures typically require elevated privileges; misconfigured privilege drop can prevent writing output files or rotating logs. ([manpages.debian.org](https://manpages.debian.org/unstable/tcpdump/tcpdump.8.en.html?utm_source=openai))
Capture visibility depends on topology (e.g., switched networks, SPAN/TAP availability) and may miss traffic not seen on the chosen interface.
Time synchronization and timestamp precision affect correlation across sensors and logs. ([man7.org](https://man7.org/linux/man-pages/man5/pcap-savefile.5.html?utm_source=openai))
Legal and privacy considerations may restrict packet capture and retention in some jurisdictions or environments.

related_tools.name: Wireshark
related_tools.relationship: GUI analyzer using libpcap/Npcap; reads/writes pcap/pcapng and complements tcpdump for deep inspection.

related_tools.name: TShark
related_tools.relationship: Wireshark’s CLI analyzer; often used alongside tcpdump for automated or headless decoding.

related_tools.name: dumpcap
related_tools.relationship: Wireshark’s capture engine optimized for high-performance capturing into files.

related_tools.name: tcptrace
related_tools.relationship: Post-processing of tcpdump/Wireshark capture files to summarize TCP connections and performance metrics. ([tcptrace.org](https://www.tcptrace.org/?utm_source=openai))

related_tools.name: editcap/mergecap
related_tools.relationship: Utilities for trimming, filtering, and merging pcap/pcapng files used in analysis pipelines.

related_tools.name: ngrep
related_tools.relationship: Payload pattern matching against packets; complements BPF’s header-oriented filtering.

related_tools.name: WinDump
related_tools.relationship: Windows port of tcpdump that uses WinPcap/Npcap for capture. ([winpcap.org](https://www.winpcap.org/windump/?utm_source=openai))

related_tools.name: libpcap/Npcap
related_tools.relationship: Underlying capture libraries providing the pcap API that tcpdump relies on. ([github.com](https://github.com/the-tcpdump-group/libpcap?utm_source=openai))

related_tools.name: netsniff-ng
related_tools.relationship: Alternative Linux toolkit for capture/replay/analysis with different performance trade-offs.

evidence_gaps: Direct access to tcpdump.org manpages and official release notes was blocked by robots via this interface, so option semantics and examples were corroborated using reputable mirrors (Debian/man7/FreeBSD) rather than the primary site. Feature availability may vary slightly by OS or tcpdump/libpcap version.
Exact current stable version as of April 7, 2026 is derived from secondary sources (e.g., Wikipedia) rather than the official site; confirm against tcpdump.org for authoritative release information.

sources.title: Debian tcpdump(8) man page (privilege drop behavior)
Npcap: Windows Packet Capture Library & Driver
